channel_id.description: The ID of the channel to archive. Example: "C1234567890"

error.description: Error message if the request failed

name.description: Name of the channel to create. Must be lowercase, contain only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores, and be 80 characters or less.

id.description: The unique identifier of the channel.

creator.description: User ID of the channel creator.

text.description: The content of the reminder. Example: "eat a banana"

time.description: When the reminder should happen. Can be a Unix timestamp or natural language like "in 5 minutes", "tomorrow at 9am"

user_id.description: The user ID to set the reminder for. If omitted, sets a reminder for the authenticated user. Note: Setting reminders for other users requires a bot token.

id.description: The unique identifier of the reminder

creator.description: The user ID of the user who created the reminder

user.description: The user ID of the user the reminder is set for

time.description: The Unix timestamp when the reminder will trigger (for non-recurring reminders)

complete_ts.description: The Unix timestamp when the reminder was completed (0 if not completed)

channel_id.description: Channel ID containing the message. Example: "C1234567890"

message_ts.description: Timestamp of the message to delete. Example: "1405894322.002768"

channel.description: The channel ID the scheduled message is posting to. Example: "C123456789"

scheduled_message_id.description: The scheduled_message_id returned from chat.scheduleMessage. Example: "Q1234ABCD"

count.description: Number of results per page. Maximum 100. Default: 20

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Use "*" for first page, then use next_cursor from response for subsequent pages

highlight.description: Enable query highlight markers in results. Default: false

sort.description: Sort matches by score or timestamp. Default: score

sort_dir.description: Sort direction: ascending or descending. Default: desc

team_id.description: Encoded team ID to search in, required if org token is used

is_mpim.description: Whether the channel is a multiparty IM

is_ext_shared.description: Whether the channel is externally shared

is_org_shared.description: Whether the channel is organization shared

next_cursor.description: Pagination cursor for the next page, or null if no more results

email.description: Email address of the user to look up. Example: "user@example.com"

channel_id.description: Slack channel ID to retrieve information about. Example: "C012AB3CD"

is_channel.description: Whether this is a public channel

is_group.description: Whether this is a private channel

is_im.description: Whether this is a direct message

is_mpim.description: Whether this is a multi-person direct message

is_general.description: Whether this is the general channel

created.description: Unix timestamp when the channel was created

creator.description: User ID of the channel creator

num_members.description: Number of members in the channel (if available)

channel_id.description: The conversation ID to fetch history for. Example: "C1234567890"

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Omit for first page.

oldest.description: Only messages after this Unix timestamp will be included. Example: "1512085950.000216"

latest.description: Only messages before this Unix timestamp will be included. Example: "1512104434.000490"

inclusive.description: Include messages with oldest or latest timestamps in results. Defaults to false.

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return (max 999). Defaults to 100.

user_id.description: User ID to fetch DND status for. If omitted, returns status for the authenticated user. Example: "U1234567890"

dnd_enabled.description: Whether Do Not Disturb is enabled

next_dnd_start_ts.description: Unix timestamp for the next DND window start. Null if no DND window scheduled

next_dnd_end_ts.description: Unix timestamp for the next DND window end. Null if no DND window scheduled

snooze_enabled.description: Whether snooze mode is currently enabled

snooze_endtime.description: Unix timestamp when snooze will end. Null if snooze is not enabled

snooze_remaining.description: Seconds remaining until snooze ends. Null if snooze is not enabled

snooze_is_indefinite.description: Whether snooze is indefinite. Null if snooze is not enabled

channel_id.description: The ID of the conversation or channel containing the message. Example: "C123ABC456"

message_ts.description: The timestamp of the message, uniquely identifying it within a channel. Example: "1358546515.000008"

permalink.description: The permalink URL for the message

channel_id.description: Channel ID where the message was posted. Example: "C1234567890"

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the message to get reactions for. Example: "1648602352.215969"

full.description: If true, always return the complete reaction list.

type.description: Type of the item (message, file, etc.). Example: "message"

channel.description: Channel ID where the message was posted

count.description: Number of users who reacted with this emoji

users.description: List of user IDs who reacted with this emoji

channel_id.description: The ID of the channel/conversation containing the thread. Example: "C1234567890"

thread_ts.description: The timestamp of the parent message in the thread. Example: "1234567890.123456"

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Omit for first page.

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return per page. Default: 100.

messages.description: Array of messages in the thread, including parent and replies

next_cursor.description: Pagination cursor for next page. Null if no more pages.

has_more.description: Whether there are more messages to fetch

filename.description: Name of the file being uploaded. Example: "document.pdf"

length.description: Size of the file in bytes. Example: 1024

alt_txt.description: Description of image for screen-reader. Only applicable for image files.

upload_url.description: The URL to upload the file content to

file_id.description: The unique file ID for this upload

user_id.description: User ID to get presence info on. Defaults to the authed user. Example: "U1234567890"

online.description: True if the user has a client currently connected to Slack

auto_away.description: True if Slack servers haven't detected activity in the last 10 minutes

manual_away.description: True if the user has manually set their presence to away

last_activity.description: Last activity seen by Slack servers (Unix timestamp)

user_id.description: User ID to get profile information for. Example: "U0123456789"

channel_id.description: The ID of the public or private channel to invite user(s) to. Example: "C024BE91L"

user_ids.description: Array of user IDs to invite to the channel. Up to 1000 users may be invited at once. Example: ["U024BE7LH", "U12345678"]

force.description: When set to true and multiple user IDs are provided, continue inviting the valid ones while ignoring invalid IDs. Defaults to false.

channel_id.description: ID of the channel to join. Example: C061EG9SL
The ID of the channel to leave. Example: "C1234567890"

error.description: Error message if the request failed

channel_id.description: Slack channel ID to list members for. Example: "C0123456789"

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Omit for first page.

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return. Default is 100, max is 1000.

members.description: List of user IDs belonging to the conversation members

next_cursor.description: Pagination cursor for next page, or null if no more results

types.description: Comma-separated list of conversation types to filter by. Options: public_channel, private_channel, mpim, im. Default: public_channel.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Omit for first page.

limit.description: Maximum number of conversations to return (1-200). Default: 100.

include_categories.description: Include a list of categories for Unicode emoji and the emoji in each category

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Omit for first page.

channel.description: Channel ID where the item is pinned

items.description: List of pinned items in the channel

channel_id.description: The channel ID to filter scheduled messages by. Example: "C123456789"

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response. Omit for first page.

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return. Default: 100

oldest.description: Unix timestamp of the earliest scheduled message to include

latest.description: Unix timestamp of the latest scheduled message to include

include_disabled.description: Include results for disabled User Groups

include_count.description: Include the number of users in each User Group

include_users.description: Include the list of users for each User Group

next_cursor.description: Pagination cursor for the next page. Null if no more pages.

channel_id.description: The channel ID to mark as read. Example: "C02MB5ZABA7"

message_ts.description: Timestamp of the message to mark as read. Example: "1234567890.123456"

user_ids.description: User IDs to open a direct message with. For a 1:1 DM, provide a single user ID. For a multi-person DM, provide multiple user IDs. Example: ["U1234567890"]

channel_id.description: The ID of the opened DM channel

channel_name.description: The name of the channel (for multi-person DMs this will be a generated name)

channel_id.description: Channel ID where the message was posted. Example: "C1234567890"

message_timestamp.description: Timestamp of the message to pin. Example: "1355517523.000005"

ok.description: Whether the pin was successfully added

channel.description: Channel, private group, or IM channel ID to send message to. Example: "C1234567890"

text.description: Text of the message to send

thread_ts.description: Timestamp of parent message to reply in thread. Example: "1234567890.123456"

channel.description: ID of the channel the message was sent to

bot_id.description: ID of the bot if sent by bot

message.description: The message object that was sent

channel_id.description: Channel ID where the message was posted. Example: "C1234567890"

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the message to remove the reaction from. Example: "1234567890.123456"

reaction_name.description: Name of the emoji reaction to remove. Example: "thumbsup"

error.description: Error message if the operation failed

channel_id.description: The ID of the channel to remove the user from. Example: "C1234567890"

user_id.description: The ID of the user to remove from the channel. Example: "U1234567890"

channel_id.description: The ID of the channel to rename. Example: "C0123456789"

channel_name.description: The new name for the channel. Names must be 80 characters or less, and can only contain lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores. Example: "general"

created.description: Unix timestamp when the channel was created

creator.description: User ID of the channel creator

is_general.description: Whether this is the general channel

is_member.description: Whether the caller is a member

num_members.description: Number of members in the channel

channel_id.description: The channel to post to. Example: "C02MB5ZABA7"

post_at.description: Unix timestamp for when to post. Example: 1735689600

thread_ts.description: Optional thread timestamp to post in a thread. Example: "1234567890.123456"

count.description: Number of items to return per page. Max 100. Default: 20

page.description: Page number of results to return. Default: 1

sort.description: Sort by score or timestamp. Default: score

sort_dir.description: Sort direction: ascending or descending. Default: desc

highlight.description: Enable query highlight markers in results. Default: false

channel_id.description: Channel ID to send the ephemeral message to. Example: "C1234567890"

user_id.description: User ID to send the ephemeral message to. The user must be in the specified channel. Example: "U1234567890"

text.description: Text of the message to send. Supports Slack formatting.

thread_ts.description: Thread timestamp to reply to a specific thread. Example: "1234567890.123456"

channel_id.description: Channel ID to send the message to. Example: "C1234567890"

text.description: Text content of the message to send. Example: "Hello world"

ts.description: Timestamp ID of the sent message

channel_id.description: Channel ID to set the purpose for. Example: "C1234567890"

purpose.description: The new purpose text for the channel.

success.description: Whether the purpose was successfully updated

channel_id.description: The ID of the channel that was updated

purpose.description: The new purpose that was set

channel_id.description: The ID of the channel to set the topic for. Example: "C12345678"

topic.description: The new topic string. Does not support formatting or linkification. Example: "Apply topically for best effects"

status_text.description: The displayed text of up to 100 characters. We strongly encourage brevity.

status_emoji.description: The displayed emoji that is enabled for the Slack team, such as `:train:` or `:coffee:`.

status_expiration.description: The Unix timestamp of when the status will expire. Providing 0 or omitting this field results in a custom status that will not expire.

presence.description: User presence status. Use "online" to set presence to auto (active) or "away" to set presence to away.

ok.description: Whether the presence was set successfully

error.description: Error message if the request failed

channel_id.description: The channel ID to unarchive. Example: "C02MB5ZABA7"

ok.description: Whether the unarchive request was successful

channel_id.description: The channel ID to unpin the message from. Example: "C1234567890"

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the message to unpin. Example: "1234567890.123456"

error.description: Error message if the request failed

channel_id.description: The ID of the channel containing the message to update. Example: "C1234567890"

message_ts.description: The timestamp of the message to update. Example: "1401383885.000061"

text.description: The updated text of the message. Example: "Updated message text"

as_user.description: Pass true to update the message as the authenticated user. Bot users in this context are considered authed users. Default: true

link_names.description: Find and link channel names and usernames. Defaults to false. To use this, you need parse set to "full".

parse.description: Change how messages are treated. Defaults to "client" which attempts to discover links. Use "none" to treat text literally, "full" for full parsing with link_names.

unfurl_links.description: Pass false to disable unfurling of links.

unfurl_media.description: Pass false to disable unfurling of media content.

reply_broadcast.description: Used to reply to a thread only and not to the channel. Pass true to reply to the channel as well.

blocks.description: A JSON array of blocks to use as the message content. When blocks is provided, text becomes the fallback text for notifications.

attachments.description: A JSON array of legacy attachments. Not recommended for new apps, use blocks instead.

ok.description: Whether the API call was successful

channel.description: The ID of the channel where the message was updated

ts.description: The timestamp of the updated message

text.description: The updated text of the message

message.description: Full message object containing updated message details
